steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$\frac{25x}{x^2-25}=\frac{x}{x-5}+\frac{20}{x+5}$$Graph it and use the visible intercept, root, or vertex the question asks for. The requested value is $10$.

steps.title: Solve the equation
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $x^2-25=(x-5)(x+5)$: $$25x=x(x+5)+20(x-5).$$ Simplify to $x^2=100$, so $x=\pm 10$.

steps.title: Identify the positive solution
steps.content: Take the positive root (and check it doesn't make a denominator zero). The answer is 10.
